codeReview.summary: The assessment-take-form feature has been successfully implemented with comprehensive support for the required functionality. The route '/assessments/:id/take' exists and is properly registered in routeTree.gen.ts. The implementation includes all required question type rendering (single_choice, multiple_choice, short_text), navigation between questions, auto-save functionality, and manual submission.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md documents a navigation pattern recommending useRouter hook over window.location.href, but the assessment take form uses window.location.href for redirects. This is a minor inconsistency but does not block functionality. All routes in the codebase follow this same pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/routes/assessments.$id.take.tsx:293 (handleCancel), line 285-289 (success redirect) use window.location.href. Compare to AGENTS.md 'Navigation Pattern' which suggests router.navigate({ to: '/partners' }).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the fullstack-worker skill procedure correctly: read context files, ran baseline commands (typecheck passed), implemented server functions and React component, and committed changes. The handoff indicates successful verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows typecheck passed (exit code 0), test passed (1 test), and commit c834b9c was created successfully.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services or commands observed. Worker used standard pnpm run dev command from services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml already contains web service on port 3002.

summary: The assessment-take-form feature has been successfully implemented. The route /assessments/:id/take exists (registered as AssessmentsIdTakeRoute in routeTree.gen.ts), loads questions from the question_bank via getTakeAssessment server function, correctly renders single_choice (radio buttons), multiple_choice (checkboxes), and short_text (text input) question types. Navigation between questions with prev/next buttons works correctly, a progress bar shows current position, and both auto-save (with 1-second debounce) and manual submit functionality are implemented. The implementation follows the coding conventions with Indonesian UI labels (e.g., 'Sebelumnya', 'Berikutnya', 'Submit Penilaian'). No blocking issues found.
